guidance.investor_verdict: Despite record FY26 revenue of ₹2,153.69 crore (+11.34%), a 6.2% YoY decline in net profit and margin compression to 16.50% EBITDA margin triggered a ~19% stock price drop post-results

analyst_dimensions.valuation_context: Stock fell ~19% to ₹10,100 on results day; analyst targets range ₹8,000-10,500, with current trailing P/E of 32.75 and EV/EBITDA of 23.34 reflecting a valuation reset post weak Q4
analyst_dimensions.consensus_gap: Q4 operating profit declined 30% YoY to ₹79.77 crore against a backdrop of record annual revenue, indicating results likely missed margin expectations despite top-line resilience
analyst_dimensions.achievability: FY27 outlook described as 'constructive' with industrial capex cycle continuing; order backlog of ₹1,200 crore plus April 2026 fresh orders of ₹310 crore support near-term revenue visibility
analyst_dimensions.margin_quality: FY26 margin decline driven by one-time Labour Code provisions, rising input costs, and negative mark-to-market gains - partly one-off (Labour Code) but input cost pressure could persist
analyst_dimensions.capex_roi: ₹25 crore Vadodara land purchase and new power transformer facility (commissioning July 2026) funded entirely via internal accruals; specific ROIC not disclosed
analyst_dimensions.order_book_health: Order backlog of ~₹1,200 crore (10,270 MVA) at FY27 start, plus ₹310 crore (2,107 MVA) added in April 2026; execution timeline not disclosed, but selective order intake suggests quality over volume
analyst_dimensions.risk_opportunity_scorecard: Opportunity: new power transformer facility commissioning July 2026 to diversify product mix and capture India capex cycle (Medium probability, High impact). Risk: persistent input cost inflation and margin compression (Medium-High probability, Medium impact)
analyst_dimensions.management_credibility: Management transparently disclosed the specific drivers of Q4 margin pressure (Labour Code provisions, input costs, MTM losses) rather than masking the profit decline, suggesting transparent reporting
analyst_dimensions.forward_estimate_impact: 6.2% FY26 net profit decline (EPS ₹301.85 vs ₹321.65 in FY25) likely pressures near-term FY27 EPS estimates downward despite revenue growth, pending margin recovery post new facility commissioning
analyst_dimensions.quarterly_trajectory: FY27 trajectory hinges on July 2026 power transformer facility commissioning as a margin-diversification catalyst, alongside order backlog execution and input cost normalization

financial.revenue: Q4 FY26 revenue ₹8,480 crore, up 111.8% YoY; FY26 full year ₹26,537 crore, up 83.7% YoY
financial.growth: FY27 EBITDA guidance of ₹7,000-7,700 crore, implying ~18-30% growth over FY26 operating EBITDA of ₹5,908 crore
financial.margins: FY26 operating EBITDA margin 22.27% (vs FY27 guidance range implying some compression); Q4 FY26 EBITDA margin 18.59%, down from prior quarters due to cost pressures
financial.capex: ~₹30,000 crore planned capex across verticals to fuel next phase of growth, including major investments in U.S. manufacturing

business.expansion: Total module manufacturing capacity ~26 GW (largest non-Chinese module manufacturer globally); cell manufacturing at 5.4 GW (largest in India); major U.S. expansion investments highlighted
business.products: Solar PV modules (core, ₹24,133 crore of FY26 revenue, +86.2% YoY), cells, EPC services, retail (B2C)
business.geography: Revenue mix: utility/IPP projects ~34.7%, overseas markets ~33%, retail ~20.8%, EPC ~11.6% of FY26 revenue

commitments: FY27 operating EBITDA guidance of ₹7,000-7,700 crore
~₹30,000 crore capex plan across verticals including U.S. expansion
risks: Higher silver, copper and freight costs pressuring Q4 margins - Medium
Lower overseas mix and cell procurement from outside sources to meet DCR demand - Medium
Working capital intensity (negative FCF from inventory/receivables growth) - Medium

guidance.summary: FY26 revenue rose 84% YoY to ₹26,537 crore with PAT more than doubling to ₹3,884 crore, beating prior EBITDA guidance of ₹5,500-6,000 crore with actual ₹6,617 crore reported EBITDA

deal_wins: Not disclosed with specific deal-level breakdown

order_book.execution_timeline: Pipeline exceeding 100 GW providing medium-term revenue visibility; specific % execution timeline not disclosed

deals_and_pipeline.sales_pipeline: Pipeline exceeding 100 GW; conversion rate not disclosed

revenue_by_segment.Solar PV Modules: ₹24,133 crore in FY26 (up from ₹12,957 crore in FY25), +86.2% YoY - dominant segment
revenue_by_segment.Retail (B2C): ₹5,515 crore in FY26, +84% YoY, ~20.8% of revenue
revenue_by_segment.Utility/IPP, Overseas, EPC: Utility/IPP ~34.7%, Overseas ~33%, EPC ~11.6% of FY26 revenue

segments.segment_outlook: Module manufacturing remains dominant growth driver; retail (B2C) showing strong traction (+84% YoY); overseas mix declined in Q4 due to DCR-driven domestic cell procurement shifts

geography.expansion_plans: Major U.S. manufacturing expansion highlighted as part of ~₹30,000 crore capex plan; specific timeline not disclosed

raw_materials.cost_outlook: Intensifying - higher silver, copper and freight costs cited as key Q4 margin pressures
raw_materials.key_materials: Silver and copper costs rose, contributing to margin pressure; freight costs also elevated

working_capital.cash_release_potential: Not disclosed - inventory/receivables growth currently driving negative free cash flow, opposite of a release

capex_detailed.facility_expansion: Major investments in U.S. manufacturing; 26 GW module / 5.4 GW cell capacity already established domestically
capex_detailed.funding_source: Not explicitly broken down by %; equity base nearly doubled (₹9,595 crore to ₹15,011 crore) via retained earnings, indicating significant self-funding

debt_management.current_net_debt: Total borrowings ₹2,491 crore (₹815 crore + ₹1,676 crore) vs shareholders' equity of ₹15,011 crore - low leverage

market_share.by_segment: Module manufacturing: largest non-Chinese manufacturer globally at ~26 GW capacity; cell manufacturing: largest in India at 5.4 GW
market_share.overall_trend: Strengthening - capacity leadership maintained and expanding via U.S. investments

competitive_position.pricing_strategy: Not explicitly disclosed; DCR-driven domestic cell sourcing shift suggests some pricing/sourcing flexibility to meet compliance requirements
competitive_advantages: Largest non-Chinese module manufacturer globally (~26 GW capacity)
Largest cell manufacturing facility in India (5.4 GW, fully operational)
Strong ROCE (32.4%) and ROE (29%), with low leverage (₹2,491 crore debt vs ₹15,011 crore equity)
competitive_threats: Rising silver, copper and freight costs compressing margins - Medium
Negative free cash flow from inventory/receivables buildup creating working capital intensity risk - Medium
Lower overseas revenue mix in Q4 due to DCR compliance sourcing shifts - Low-Medium

guidance.investor_verdict: Waaree delivered a record FY26 with revenue up 84% to ₹26,537 crore and EBITDA beating guidance (₹6,617 crore vs ₹5,500-6,000 crore guided), but FY27 EBITDA guidance of ₹7,000-7,700 crore implies decelerating growth amid margin pressure and working capital concerns

analyst_dimensions.valuation_context: Stock traded at ₹2,200 against a 52-week high of ₹3,755 (1-year return -38%) ahead of results, with analyst targets of ₹3,050-3,200, suggesting significant valuation re-rating potential if FY27 guidance is met
analyst_dimensions.consensus_gap: Q4 PAT of ₹1,126 crore (+75% YoY) and revenue of ₹8,480 crore beat analyst estimates of ₹4,500-5,000 crore revenue and ₹440-510 crore PAT by a wide margin
analyst_dimensions.achievability: Prior FY26 EBITDA guidance of ₹5,500-6,000 crore was exceeded (₹6,617 crore actual), demonstrating a track record of beating guidance, though FY27 guidance of ₹7,000-7,700 crore implies a lower growth rate than FY26's 117% EBITDA growth
analyst_dimensions.margin_quality: FY26 margin expansion to 22.27% was strong, but Q4 margins (18.59%) were hurt by higher silver, copper and freight costs plus DCR-driven sourcing shifts - sustainability depends on commodity cost trends
analyst_dimensions.capex_roi: ~₹30,000 crore capex plan supported by strong ROCE (32.4%) and ROE (29%) and low leverage, suggesting capacity for value-accretive investment, though specific project-level ROIC not disclosed
analyst_dimensions.order_book_health: Order book of ~₹53,000 crore backed by pipeline exceeding 100 GW provides strong medium-term revenue visibility; execution timeline and segment breakdown not disclosed, posing some transparency risk
analyst_dimensions.risk_opportunity_scorecard: Opportunity: FY27 EBITDA guidance of ₹7,000-7,700 crore plus ₹53,000 crore order book and 100 GW pipeline (High probability given beat track record, High impact). Risk: working capital intensity causing negative FCF and rising input costs (Medium probability, Medium impact)
analyst_dimensions.management_credibility: Management exceeded its own FY26 EBITDA guidance (₹6,617 crore vs ₹5,500-6,000 crore guided) and proactively flagged Q4 margin pressures (commodity costs, DCR sourcing), indicating a track record of conservative guidance-setting with transparent disclosure
analyst_dimensions.forward_estimate_impact: FY27 EBITDA guidance of ₹7,000-7,700 crore (vs FY26 actual ₹6,617 crore reported) implies modest 6-16% EBITDA growth, a sharp deceleration from FY26's 117% growth, likely moderating FY27 EPS growth estimates
analyst_dimensions.quarterly_trajectory: FY27 trajectory depends on working capital cycle normalization, CWIP commissioning (U.S. and domestic capacity), and commodity cost trends (silver, copper, freight) as key catalysts and risks

financial.revenue: Q4 FY26 net sales ₹589.19 crore, up 69.21% YoY and 33.10% QoQ (vs ₹442.68 crore in Q3 FY26); FY26 consolidated revenue ₹798.35 crore / standalone ₹1,856 crore depending on basis, up ~45% YoY
financial.growth: FY27 revenue guidance revised to ₹2,400+ crore (from prior ₹2,000 crore minimum); management indicated high probability of further upgrade given strong order inflow
financial.margins: Q4 FY26 PAT margin 12.25%, down from 12.72% in Q3 FY26 and 15.23% in Q4 FY25; gross margins expected to revert to historical 33-34% after one-off Turkey LD penalty hit consolidated margins by ~300 bps
financial.capex: FY26 capex of ₹33.0 crore (partial figure disclosed); third manufacturing plant ramping up, >30% operational, to support capacity expansion

business.expansion: Third manufacturing plant ramping up (>30% operational); capacity additions expected to enable revenue potential up to ₹3,200 crore (INR32B) by FY28
business.products: Generators and electric motors; segments include Steam Turbines, Gas Turbine/Gas Engine, Hydro; company also described as DG sets/captive power plant manufacturer
business.geography: Export sales accounted for 66% of H1 sales (high export focus); Turkey plant operations also referenced

commitments: FY27 revenue guidance of ₹2,400+ crore with high probability of further upgrade
All ~₹2,000 crore in pending orders to be executed within FY27 per management (Nikhil)
risks: One-off liquidated damages penalty in Turkey impacted consolidated margins by ~300 bps - Medium
Receivables grew 70% YoY to ₹742 crore, not discussed on call - Medium
US tariffs may impact 4-5% of sales, partially mitigated by Turkey plant - Low-Medium

guidance.summary: Q4 FY26 net sales hit an all-time high of ₹589.19 crore (+69.21% YoY), with FY26 order inflow of ₹2,238 crore (+51% YoY) supporting raised FY27 revenue guidance of ₹2,400+ crore

deal_wins: Gas Turbine and Gas Engine large volume orders including deliveries to projects like SpaceX, FY26
Q4 FY26 order inflow of ₹666 crore (+61% QoQ), a record quarter

order_book.total_value: Consolidated order book ~₹1,508.5 crore as of March 31, 2026; manufacturing order book at ₹1,973 crore (per integrated analysis) providing ~14 months forward visibility
order_book.execution_timeline: Management stated all ~₹2,000 crore in pending orders will be executed within FY27 (this fiscal year)
order_book.by_segment: Not disclosed - management declined to provide segment breakdowns beyond steam turbines on the call

customer_additions: Not disclosed by name beyond reference to large gas turbine/engine orders including SpaceX-linked projects

revenue_by_segment.Gas Turbine/Gas Engine: Described as experiencing 'massive growth' with large volume orders including SpaceX; specific % revenue split not disclosed
revenue_by_segment.Hydro: FY27 projected to be the highest in company history for hydro; specific % revenue split not disclosed

geography.expansion_plans: Export growth to continue driving FY27/FY28 business; Turkey plant helps mitigate US tariff exposure (4-5% of sales at risk)

headcount.hiring_plan: Management stated they have 'largely employed everyone we want to' with no major incremental hiring expected, only minor increases
headcount.utilization_target: Third plant >30% operational; no capacity constraints expected, with room to expand further if needed

raw_materials.expected_benefit: Gross margin normalization back to 33-34% range from compressed levels (specific bps not disclosed for Q4)
raw_materials.key_materials: Copper cost increases noted as being passed through to customers via price renegotiations

capex_detailed.total: ₹33.0 crore (fixed assets rose from ₹168.53 crore to ₹190.08 crore as of March 2025)
capex_detailed.facility_expansion: Third manufacturing plant ramping up (>30% operational), supporting capacity for revenue potential up to ₹3,200 crore by FY28

market_share.by_segment: Not disclosed with specific share figures by segment
market_share.overall_trend: Strengthening - record quarterly revenue, record order inflow (+51% YoY), and raised FY27/FY28 guidance

competitive_position.pricing_strategy: Commodity cost increases (copper) successfully passed through to customers via renegotiation, indicating reasonable pricing power; rupee depreciation against EUR/USD also expected to benefit margins after stopping FX hedging ~6 months prior
competitive_advantages: 79% export-driven order inflow (₹1,572 crore in Q3, +48% YoY) with strong global demand visibility
Third manufacturing plant ramp-up removes near-term capacity constraints, with room for further expansion
Diversified high-growth end markets: AI data centers, grid stabilization, renewables, and large gas turbine/engine orders (e.g., SpaceX)
competitive_threats: Receivables growth of 70% YoY to ₹742 crore not addressed on the call - Medium

US tariffs potentially impacting 4-5% of sales - Low-Medium

guidance.investor_verdict: TD Power Systems delivered record Q4 FY26 revenue of ₹589.19 crore (+69.21% YoY) and FY26 order inflow of ₹2,238 crore (+51% YoY), prompting management to raise FY27 revenue guidance to ₹2,400+ crore with a high probability of further upgrades

analyst_dimensions.valuation_context: Stock trades at a premium valuation (P/E ~64x per analysis), justifiable only if growth and margins sustain through FY30 based on the strong demand outlook across gas turbines, hydro, and data center-driven markets
analyst_dimensions.consensus_gap: Analyst Q4 FY26 estimates were revenue ₹265-288 crore and PAT ₹46-54 crore; actual results of ₹589.19 crore revenue and ₹72.19 crore PAT substantially beat these estimates
analyst_dimensions.achievability: FY26 revenue guidance of ₹1,800 crore was tracked on-target through Q3 (execution ahead of plan, 18.8% margin vs 18% guided); Q4 FY26 order inflow alone (₹666 crore, annualized ₹2,664 crore) already exceeds the new FY27 guidance of ₹2,400+ crore, suggesting guidance is conservative and achievable
analyst_dimensions.margin_quality: Margin compression (12.25% PAT margin in Q4 vs 15.23% in Q4 FY25) driven by a one-off Turkey LD penalty (~300 bps) and product mix/operational deleverage; gross margins expected to normalize to historical 33-34% range, suggesting underlying margin quality remains sound
analyst_dimensions.capex_roi: Capex of ₹33.0 crore supporting third plant ramp-up (>30% operational) which enables revenue potential up to ₹3,200 crore by FY28 - specific ROIC not disclosed but capacity utilization appears to justify investment given order inflow trends
analyst_dimensions.order_book_health: Order book of ₹1,508.5-1,973 crore (depending on basis) provides ~14 months forward visibility; FY26 order inflow of ₹2,238 crore was 21% above FY26 revenue, indicating a growing backlog and strong execution risk is low given management's explicit commitment to execute pending orders within FY27
analyst_dimensions.risk_opportunity_scorecard: Opportunity: FY27 guidance of ₹2,400+ crore likely conservative given Q4 inflow run-rate already exceeding it, with high probability of upgrade (High probability, High impact). Risk: rising receivables (₹742 crore, +70% YoY) and negative operating cash flow trend from working capital build-up (Medium probability, Medium impact)
analyst_dimensions.management_credibility: Management has a recent track record of meeting/exceeding guidance (FY26 revenue and margin delivery ahead of plan in Q3) and proactively flagged the one-off Turkey penalty and FX hedging strategy changes, though they explicitly declined segment-level disclosure - suggesting selective transparency
analyst_dimensions.forward_estimate_impact: FY27 revenue guidance raised to ₹2,400+ crore (from prior ₹2,000 crore minimum) with explicit signal of further potential upgrades, likely driving upward revisions to FY27 EPS estimates; FY28 revenue potential flagged at up to ₹3,200 crore
analyst_dimensions.quarterly_trajectory: Production targeted to ramp to ₹550-575 crore in Q4-26 and then ~₹600 crore per quarter from Q1-27 onwards as the third plant fully ramps; margin normalization to 33-34% gross margin expected in coming quarters as the one-off Turkey impact fades

financial.revenue: Q4 FY26 revenue ₹15,962 crore; FY26 full year revenue ₹63,681 crore
financial.growth: FY26 EBITDA up 11% YoY to ₹16,090 crore; specific FY27 revenue growth target not disclosed, but FY27 capex reaffirmed at ₹25,000 crore
financial.margins: Q4 FY26 EBITDA ₹4,216 crore, up 10% YoY; specific operating/net margin percentages not disclosed
financial.capex: FY26 actual capex came in at ₹13,000 crore, sharply below the ~₹20,000-22,000 crore guided in November, due to delays in large utility-scale solar, wind and transmission line projects; FY27 capex reaffirmed at ₹25,000 crore

business.expansion: Commissioned 2.5 GW of renewable capacity in FY26, expanding total renewable portfolio to 11.6 GW including under-construction projects; solar manufacturing produced 3,825 MW modules and 3,759 MW cells at Tirunelveli, Tamil Nadu
business.products: Renewables (generation, rooftop solar, module/cell manufacturing), conventional generation (including Mundra), transmission & distribution (including Odisha DISCOMs)
business.geography: Not disclosed by region/state breakdown beyond Odisha DISCOM and Tirunelveli (Tamil Nadu) manufacturing references

commitments: Reaffirmed FY27 capex guidance of ₹25,000 crore, with strong growth from renewables, distribution, and manufacturing
Disciplined approach to balance sheet management
risks: FY26 capex came in sharply below prior guidance (~₹13,000 crore actual vs ~₹20,000-22,000 crore guided) due to project execution delays - Medium-High
Mundra plant experienced a nine-month outage impacting FY26 EBITDA - Medium
Delays specifically in large utility-scale solar, wind, and transmission line projects - Medium

guidance.summary: FY26 PAT crossed ₹5,000 crore for the first time (up 7% YoY to ₹5,118 crore) with EBITDA up 11% YoY to ₹16,090 crore, despite a nine-month Mundra outage and capex significantly trailing guidance

deal_wins: Not disclosed with specific deal-level ₹ breakdowns

customer_additions: Cumulative rooftop solar installations crossed 3.7 lakh systems with installed capacity exceeding 4.8 GWp (FY26)

revenue_by_segment.Renewables: FY26 PAT up 59% YoY to ₹1,994 crore; revenue % split not disclosed
revenue_by_segment.Solar Manufacturing (Tirunelveli): FY26 PAT doubled to ₹857 crore on production of 3,825 MW modules and 3,759 MW cells; revenue % split not disclosed
revenue_by_segment.Rooftop Solar: FY26 PAT up 150% YoY to ₹499 crore; revenue % split not disclosed

segments.segment_outlook: Renewables, rooftop solar, and solar manufacturing all accelerating strongly (PAT growth of 59%, 150%, and 100% respectively); conventional generation (Mundra) was a drag due to a nine-month outage

geography.expansion_plans: Not disclosed beyond renewable capacity commissioning targets

capex_detailed.total: FY26 actual capex ~₹13,000 crore (vs ~₹20,000-22,000 crore guided); FY27 guidance reaffirmed at ₹25,000 crore
capex_detailed.facility_expansion: Renewables, distribution, and manufacturing flagged as key FY27 capex growth areas

market_share.by_segment: Not disclosed with specific share figures
market_share.overall_trend: Strengthening in renewables, rooftop solar and manufacturing (record PAT growth); conventional generation weaker due to Mundra outage

competitive_advantages: Diversified portfolio across renewables (11.6 GW), rooftop solar (4.8 GWp installed), and domestic solar module/cell manufacturing (Tirunelveli)
22nd consecutive quarter of profit growth track record (as of FY25/FY26 results)
Improved Odisha DISCOM performance contributing to T&D segment strength
competitive_threats: Significant capex execution shortfall vs guidance (FY26 actual ~₹13,000 crore vs ~₹20,000-22,000 crore guided) - Medium-High
Mundra nine-month outage indicates conventional generation reliability risk - Medium
Project delays specifically in utility-scale solar/wind/transmission projects could recur - Medium

guidance.investor_verdict: Tata Power crossed ₹5,000 crore PAT for the first time in FY26 (₹5,118 crore, +7% YoY) and EBITDA grew 11% to ₹16,090 crore despite a Mundra outage, but FY26 capex execution fell well short of guidance, raising questions about achievability of the reaffirmed ₹25,000 crore FY27 capex target

analyst_dimensions.consensus_gap: Q4 FY26 revenue of ₹15,962 crore came in below the analyst estimate range of ₹16,500-17,500 crore; Q4 PAT of ₹1,416 crore (+8% YoY) was a more modest beat relative to the strong renewables-driven full-year PAT growth
analyst_dimensions.achievability: FY27 capex guidance of ₹25,000 crore was reaffirmed despite FY26 actual capex of only ~₹13,000 crore against a similar prior guidance of ~₹20,000-22,000 crore, raising material questions about achievability given a recurring pattern of project execution delays
analyst_dimensions.margin_quality: FY26 EBITDA growth (+11%) was driven primarily by renewables, rooftop solar, and solar manufacturing PAT gains (+59%, +150%, +100% respectively), which appear structural and capacity-driven; Mundra outage was a one-off drag that should reverse in FY27
analyst_dimensions.capex_roi: Not disclosed - specific ROIC not provided, though renewables and manufacturing segments showing strong PAT growth suggest positive returns on deployed capacity
analyst_dimensions.risk_opportunity_scorecard: Opportunity: renewables/rooftop/manufacturing segments delivering 59-150% PAT growth with 11.6 GW portfolio scale (High probability, High impact). Risk: repeated capex guidance misses (FY25 ₹16,200 crore vs plan, FY26 ~₹13,000 crore vs ~₹20,000-22,000 crore guided) signal persistent execution challenges in utility-scale projects (High probability, Medium-High impact)
analyst_dimensions.management_credibility: Management has now twice significantly missed capex guidance (FY25 actual ₹16,200 crore, FY26 actual ~₹13,000 crore, both below respective targets), attributing both to large utility-scale project delays - this pattern suggests guidance has been historically aggressive relative to execution capability
analyst_dimensions.forward_estimate_impact: FY26 PAT of ₹5,118 crore (record high) supports near-term EPS estimates, but the capex shortfall and reaffirmed but unproven FY27 ₹25,000 crore target introduce uncertainty into longer-term renewable capacity growth assumptions underpinning FY27/FY28 estimates
analyst_dimensions.quarterly_trajectory: FY27 trajectory depends on Mundra returning to normal operations (removing the nine-month outage drag), continued renewables/rooftop/manufacturing PAT momentum, and whether the ₹25,000 crore FY27 capex guidance proves more achievable than the FY26 target it replaced

financial.revenue: Q4 FY26 revenue ₹5,468 crore, up 44.9% YoY; FY26 full year revenue ₹16,679 crore, up 53.7-54% YoY
financial.growth: FY26 EBITDA grew 63% YoY to ₹3,022 crore, PBT up 67% to ₹2,422 crore; specific FY27 revenue/EBITDA growth target not disclosed in ₹ terms, but industry installations expected to reach 8-9 GW in FY27
financial.margins: Q4 FY26 EBITDA margin 17.6% (stable vs ₹693 crore/Q4 FY25); FY26 full-year EBITDA margin expanded to 18.10% from 17.10% in FY25
financial.capex: Not disclosed - no specific capex amount or purpose disclosed

business.expansion: EPC offering expanded to 28% of order book in H2 FY26; Andhra Pradesh project progress with 2.1 GW development rights and PPA extension for 775 MW; OMS business manages over 15.7 GW in India
business.products: Wind Turbine Generators (S144 platform dominant, ~9 GW cumulative order intake), EPC contracts, O&M services (OMS)
business.geography: Primarily India-focused; record India deliveries of 2,456 MW for FY26 (highest-ever)

commitments: Continued positive momentum in deliveries and order book expected, supported by cost management and supply chain optimization
EPC transition continuing as a strategic growth driver despite taking longer than expected
risks: Order inflow for Q4 appeared muted, raising concerns about declines in coming quarters - Medium
EPC transition taking longer than expected, potentially impacting short-term order book growth - Medium
Uncertainty regarding impact of DSM (Deviation Settlement Mechanism) changes on operations - Medium

guidance.summary: FY26 revenue grew 54% YoY to ₹16,679 crore with EBITDA up 63% to ₹3,022 crore and PAT rising to ₹3,163 crore, backed by record deliveries of 2,456 MW and a 5.9 GW order book

deal_wins: S144 wind turbine platform achieved cumulative order intake of ~9 GW as of Q4 FY26
Andhra Pradesh project: 2.1 GW development rights secured plus PPA extension for 775 MW

order_book.total_value: Order book of ~5.9 GW (not disclosed in ₹ Cr)
order_book.by_segment: 66% of order book from C&I (Commercial & Industrial) and PSU segments; EPC contracts grew to 28% of order book in H2 FY26

deals_and_pipeline.sales_pipeline: Industry demand of 8-9 GW expected in FY27 and ~10 GW in FY28; FDRE installations potentially reaching 15 GW over next five years; conversion rates not disclosed

customer_additions: Growing participation from PSU and C&I segment customers, now 66% of order book
customers.net_assessment: Net positive - diversification trend toward C&I/PSU segments (66% of order book) and EPC contracts (28% of order book) reduces reliance on traditional IPP customer base

revenue_by_segment.WTG (Wind Turbine Generator): Revenue grew 65% YoY in FY26; specific % of total revenue not disclosed
revenue_by_segment.OMS (O&M Services): Manages over 15.7 GW in India with machine availability consistently above 95%; revenue % not disclosed
revenue_by_segment.EPC: Grew to 28% of order book in H2 FY26 (order book share, not revenue share)

segments.segment_outlook: WTG segment accelerating fastest (+65% YoY revenue growth); EPC transition is a strategic growth area but progressing slower than expected; OMS remains a stable, high-availability recurring revenue base

revenue_mix.India: Dominant - record 2,456 MW FY26 deliveries, all described as India installations

geography.expansion_plans: Not disclosed beyond domestic Andhra Pradesh project expansion (2.1 GW development rights)

headcount.utilization_target: OMS machine availability consistently above 95% across 15.7+ GW managed in India

raw_materials.cost_outlook: Margins supported by cost management and supply chain optimization per management commentary; not specified as easing or intensifying

debt_management.current_net_debt: Net cash position of ₹2,384 crore as of March 31, 2026 (net cash, not net debt)

market_share.by_segment: S144 platform described as the industry's dominant product with ~9 GW cumulative order intake
market_share.overall_trend: Strengthening - record deliveries, highest-ever India installations, and dominant S144 platform position

competitive_position.pricing_strategy: Not explicitly disclosed; EBITDA margin expansion (17.10% to 18.10% FY26) suggests some pricing/cost discipline
competitive_advantages: S144 platform is the industry's dominant product with ~9 GW cumulative order intake
Record India deliveries (2,456 MW FY26, highest-ever) demonstrating execution capability
Strong net cash position of ₹2,384 crore providing financial flexibility, with OMS providing stable recurring revenue (95%+ availability across 15.7 GW)
competitive_threats: Muted Q4 order inflow raising concerns about future order book growth - Medium
EPC transition taking longer than expected vs strategic timeline - Medium
26% US reciprocal tariff on Indian goods creating macro uncertainty for the broader sector, with potential FII outflow and earnings estimate cut risks - Low-Medium (indirect)

guidance.investor_verdict: Suzlon delivered record FY26 results (revenue +54% to ₹16,679 crore, EBITDA +63% to ₹3,022 crore, PAT ₹3,163 crore) with a 5.9 GW order book and net cash of ₹2,384 crore, though muted Q4 order inflow and slower-than-expected EPC transition warrant monitoring

analyst_dimensions.valuation_context: Stock ended roughly flat (-0.47% to ₹53.50) on results day despite strong headline numbers; 1-year return of -26% prior to results suggests results may not have fully addressed prior valuation concerns
analyst_dimensions.consensus_gap: Q4 FY26 revenue of ₹5,468 crore far exceeded analyst estimates of ₹3,400-3,700 crore, a substantial beat; however Q4 PAT of ₹1,119 crore was roughly flat vs ₹1,158 crore in Q4 FY25 (which had benefited from a large deferred tax credit), so on a like-for-like basis underlying PAT growth was strong (+151% sequentially from Q3)
analyst_dimensions.achievability: FY26 delivered 60% revenue growth as previously flagged in Q3 FY26 commentary ('60% growth outlook'), demonstrating management met its own targets; FY27 industry demand of 8-9 GW provides a reasonable base, though muted Q4 order inflow is a yellow flag for near-term achievability
analyst_dimensions.margin_quality: FY26 EBITDA margin expansion (17.10% to 18.10%) driven by cost management, supply chain optimization, and scale from record deliveries - appears structural given consistent OMS availability (95%+) and growing high-margin EPC mix
analyst_dimensions.capex_roi: Not disclosed - no capex figures provided, though net cash position of ₹2,384 crore suggests capacity for self-funded growth investment if needed
analyst_dimensions.order_book_health: 5.9 GW order book with 66% from C&I/PSU segments and 28% EPC share (H2 FY26) provides good diversification and revenue visibility, though muted Q4 order inflow and slower EPC transition introduce moderate execution risk to near-term order book growth
analyst_dimensions.risk_opportunity_scorecard: Opportunity: S144 platform dominance (~9 GW order intake) and FY27/FY28 industry demand of 8-10 GW with FDRE potentially adding 15 GW over 5 years (High probability, High impact). Risk: muted Q4 order inflow and slower EPC transition could constrain near-term order book momentum (Medium probability, Medium impact)
analyst_dimensions.management_credibility: Management delivered on the 60% growth outlook flagged in the prior quarter's commentary, and transparently disclosed both the Q4 PAT comparison caveat (FY25 deferred tax credit) and emerging concerns (muted order inflow, EPC transition pace), suggesting transparent reporting
analyst_dimensions.forward_estimate_impact: Strong FY26 EPS growth (₹2.31 vs ₹1.51 in FY25, +53%) supports positive near-term estimates, though muted Q4 order inflow could temper FY27 EPS growth expectations pending clarity on order book trajectory
analyst_dimensions.quarterly_trajectory: FY27 trajectory will hinge on whether Q4's muted order inflow proves transient, the pace of EPC transition (28% of order book in H2 FY26), and execution against the 8-9 GW FY27 industry demand backdrop, with the Andhra Pradesh project (2.1 GW) as a near-term catalyst

guidance.quarter: Q4 FY26 (results approved late May 2026; post-earnings concall held ₹ detailed Q4 transcript not fully public in reviewed sources; richest guidance detail comes from the Q3 FY26 call of Feb 2026 and stands as the operative framework)

financial.revenue: FY26 guidance of ₹240 Cr was MISSED ₹ actual FY26 revenue ~₹207 Cr (+~8% YoY) due to prior BSNL/operator rollout delays; no fresh numeric FY27 revenue guide confirmed, but management called FY27 a 'game changer' on telecom capex
financial.growth: FY27 operational targets: 17,000 tenancies and 8,000 towers (vs ~6,000-6,500 towers base), riding Vodafone Idea's ₹45,000 Cr 3-year capex and BSNL's ₹28,000 Cr FY27 allocation for 23,000 new 4G sites
financial.margins: Management framework: ~70% EBITDA margin and 30-32% PAT margin 'sustained' ₹ but FY26 reported PAT was only ₹34.4 Cr (~16.6% margin), roughly half the claimed band and down sharply from FY25's ~₹70 Cr, implying exceptional items/higher depreciation-interest absorbed the difference
financial.capex: Tower-addition capex not quantified; ₹35 Cr data centre project expected to contribute from Q4 FY26

business.expansion: Tower fleet expansion toward 8,000 by FY27; new data centre vertical (~₹35 Cr project); stated ambition to be among India's top-3 telecom tower companies; IP-I registered across 12 telecom circles
business.products: Passive telecom infrastructure: ground-based/rooftop/camouflage towers, high-power small cells, poles, OFC systems; per-tower revenue improved to ₹31,533/month; new data-centre stream
business.geography: India only ₹ 12 telecom circles, Maharashtra/Mumbai-centric historically

management.tone: Positive (aggressively so, despite the FY26 miss)
management.confidence: High (management); warranting investor skepticism given the miss
commitments: Data centre project (~₹35 Cr) contribution from Q4 FY26
risks: FY26 guidance missed (₹207 Cr vs ₹240 Cr guided) on operator rollout delays ₹ the same dependency governs FY27 - High
Reported FY26 PAT (₹34.4 Cr, ~16.6% margin) is far below the claimed 30-32% band ₹ earnings quality/exceptional items need scrutiny - High
Anchor-tenant concentration in stressed counterparties (Vodafone Idea, BSNL) for both growth and receivables - High
Stock down ~65% from its Jan 2025 record (₹1,714 ₹ ~₹560), signaling broken market confidence - Medium

guidance.summary: Suyog missed its FY26 revenue guide (~₹207 Cr delivered vs ₹240 Cr promised) and reported PAT of just ₹34.4 Cr, yet doubled down with FY27 targets of 17,000 tenancies and 8,000 towers pegged to Vodafone Idea's ₹45,000 Cr and BSNL's ₹28,000 Cr capex plans.

deal_wins: Data centre project ~₹35 Cr (Q4 FY26 contribution expected)
BSNL 4G rollout participation (site counts/values not disclosed)
No individually disclosed contract values in reviewed materials

order_book.total_value: Not disclosed ₹ tenancy/master-service-agreement model rather than order book

deals_and_pipeline.sales_pipeline: Pipeline = operator capex programs: Vi ₹45,000 Cr (3 years), BSNL ₹28,000 Cr (FY27, 23,000 sites); Suyog's capture share not quantified

customer_additions: Data centre client(s) ₹ unnamed; core tenants remain Vi, BSNL/MTNL, Jio, Airtel
customers.customer_concentration: Very high ₹ a handful of telecom operators constitute virtually all revenue, with the growth thesis leaning on the two financially weakest (Vi, BSNL)
customers.net_assessment: Net cautious ₹ tenancy growth prospects are real but counterparty quality is the sector's worst pocket; data centre is an early diversification gesture

segments.segment_outlook: Tenancy additions hinge entirely on Vi/BSNL execution in FY27; data centre is nascent optionality; per-tower revenue (₹31,533) trending up is the one clean positive

geography.expansion_plans: Deeper circle penetration alongside BSNL/Vi rollouts; no specific new-circle disclosures

headcount.utilization_target: Tenancy ratio targets implied by 17,000 tenancies on 8,000 towers (~2.1x) ₹ aggressive vs industry ~1.4-1.7x

raw_materials.cost_outlook: Steel/fabrication and energy for sites; no specific commentary in reviewed materials
raw_materials.key_materials: Tower steel | power/diesel at sites | fiber

working_capital.receivables_dso: Not disclosed; operator receivables (especially BSNL/Vi) are the structural risk

capex_detailed.total: Not quantified; 8,000-tower target implies a heavy multi-hundred-crore program (~₹15-25 lakh/tower)
capex_detailed.funding_source: Internal accruals + debt (D/E ~30%); funding plan for the full 8,000-tower ambition not articulated

debt_management.current_net_debt: Debt/equity ~30% (moderate); absolute net debt not disclosed in reviewed materials

market_share.by_segment: Niche regional IP-I player (~6,000+ towers) vs Indus Towers (~230,000+), making 'top-3' ambition a multi-order-of-magnitude stretch; strong micro-position in Mumbai rooftop/small-cell sites
market_share.overall_trend: Stable niche, but FY26's miss while telecom capex accelerated suggests capture-rate weakness

competitive_position.pricing_strategy: Rental escalations built into MSAs; per-tower revenue rising to ₹31,533/month shows decent site-level economics; limited negotiating power vs giant tenants
competitive_advantages: 70% EBITDA-margin site economics with long-tenor MSA escalations
Dense Mumbai/Maharashtra rooftop and small-cell portfolio (hard-to-replicate urban locations)
Promoter skin in the game ₹ CEO directly owns ~48% (52% promoter holding)
competitive_threats: Indus Towers and operator self-builds absorbing the bulk of new-site demand (High)
Vi/BSNL financial fragility ₹ rollout delays or payment stress flow straight through (High)
5G architecture shifts (small cells/fiber-to-tower) changing economics faster than a microcap can invest (Medium)

guidance.investor_verdict: A microcap tower operator that just missed its own ₹240 Cr guide, printed PAT at half its claimed margin band, and is down 65% from peak ₹ now asking the market to underwrite a 17,000-tenancy FY27 'game changer' premised on India's two weakest telecom balance sheets spending on schedule.

analyst_dimensions.valuation_context: Mcap ~₹879-1,020 Cr on FY26 PAT ₹34.4 Cr ₹ ~25-30x trailing ₹ not cheap for a guidance-missing microcap, though only ~12-13x if the claimed 'normalized' 30% margins on ₹240 Cr+ revenue ever materialize; the market is pricing the miss, not the dream
analyst_dimensions.consensus_gap: No formal coverage; the relevant gap is management's own ₹240 Cr FY26 guide vs ₹207 Cr delivered (-14%), and claimed 30-32% PAT margins vs 16.6% reported ₹ both gaps unexplained in public detail
analyst_dimensions.achievability: Low-to-moderate: 8,000 towers/17,000 tenancies by FY27 implies ~25-30% tower growth and a 2.1x tenancy ratio in one year, dependent on Vi (chronically capex-delayed) and BSNL (execution-challenged) ₹ directionally plausible, magnitudes aggressive
analyst_dimensions.margin_quality: Site-level economics are genuinely strong (70% EBITDA, rising per-tower revenue), but the FY26 PAT collapse to ₹34.4 Cr signals below-the-line erosion (depreciation, interest, possible receivable provisioning) that management's margin narrative glosses over ₹ the single most important diligence item
analyst_dimensions.capex_roi: Tower ROI depends on tenancy ratios and tenant solvency; building ahead of Vi/BSNL demand is the classic way small tower firms impair capital; the unquantified funding plan for 8,000 towers at ~30% D/E is a gap
analyst_dimensions.order_book_health: No backlog construct; visibility = MSA escalations (good) + operator capex promises (poor reliability); the FY26 miss is direct evidence of conversion risk
analyst_dimensions.risk_opportunity_scorecard: Opportunities: BSNL 23,000-site FY27 rollout (medium prob/high impact for Suyog's size), Vi capex revival post-funding (medium/high), data centre vertical (low-medium/medium), per-tower monetization gains (high/low). Risks: another guidance miss (high/high), Vi/BSNL receivable stress (medium/high), funding strain on tower build (medium/medium), illiquid microcap drawdowns (high/medium)
analyst_dimensions.management_credibility: Weak-to-mixed ₹ a hard numeric miss within months of confident guidance, plus a margin narrative (30-32%) contradicted by reported numbers (16.6%); offset by high promoter ownership (48% CEO), dividend continuity, and clean audit opinion; require delivered quarters before trusting targets
analyst_dimensions.forward_estimate_impact: Credible FY27 base case: revenue ₹235-260 Cr (+15-25%) if BSNL/Vi rollouts partially land, PAT ₹45-60 Cr on normalization (EPS ~₹40-53) ₹ ~11-14x forward; management's implied bull case (₹300 Cr+, 30% margins) would halve that multiple but deserves heavy probability discounting
analyst_dimensions.quarterly_trajectory: Q1-Q2 FY27: tenancy-addition and tower-count disclosures are the proof metrics (need ~400-500 towers/quarter); data centre revenue recognition from Q4 FY26 onward; catalysts ₹ BSNL site-award announcements, Vi funding/capex news, quarterly PAT margin normalization evidence, AGM (dividend, MOA changes)

financial.revenue: FY27 guidance: ~20% revenue growth on FY26 revenue of ₹827.87 Cr (+18.9% YoY); Q4 FY26 revenue ₹276.53 Cr (+50.2% YoY) — highest-ever quarter
financial.growth: ~20% for FY27, driven by new launches (cardiovascular, ADHD, anesthetics), Europe CEP-approval-led customer adds, and capacity expansion runway (Patalganga)
financial.margins: FY27 EBITDA margin guided at 33-35% (FY26: ~35.5%; Q4: 35.3% vs 36.7% YoY); FY26 PAT ₹209.12 Cr (+11.3%), PAT margin ~25.3% (Q4: 26.8%); guide implies margin held roughly flat-to-slightly-lower as new capacity absorbs
financial.capex: Patalganga land — all clearances secured for the next capacity-expansion phase; capex amount/timeline not quantified in reviewed excerpts; ongoing backward-integration and finished-dosage investments

business.expansion: Patalganga greenfield site cleared for future capacity; expanding finished dosage forms beyond APIs; backward integration deepening; Europe expansion via new CEP approvals
business.products: Niche API leader: anesthetics (core, strong FY26 driver), antihistamines/anti-allergics, cardiovascular (new, fast-growing), ADHD products (new), vitamins, anti-hypertensives; moving up the value chain into formulations
business.geography: Exports 82% of FY26 revenue; Europe 14% of revenue and growing; LatAm/Asia significant (historic strongholds); regulated-market focus explicit strategy

commitments: ~20% revenue growth with 33-35% EBITDA margin in FY27
Expand finished dosage forms and backward integration
Maintain regulated-market compliance edge (USFDA inspection closed with one minor observation)
risks: Working capital days rose to 170 from 158 — cash conversion deteriorating - Medium
Q4's +50% included favorable timing; full-year growth was 19%, so extrapolating Q4 is dangerous - Medium
Margin guide (33-35%) concedes possible compression vs FY26's 35.5% - Low/Medium
API pricing/China competition cyclicality in key molecules - Medium

guidance.summary: Supriya guided ~20% FY27 growth at 33-35% EBITDA margins after a blowout Q4 (revenue ₹276.5 Cr, +50%; PAT ₹74.2 Cr, +47%) capped FY26 at record revenue of ₹827.9 Cr — sending the stock up 20% to the upper circuit.

deal_wins: Not applicable — API contract/spot model; notable wins are regulatory: new European CEP approvals and new customer acquisitions in Europe (names/values not disclosed)

order_book.total_value: Not disclosed — API businesses don't report order books

deals_and_pipeline.sales_pipeline: Product pipeline is the proxy: cardiovascular and ADHD launches ramping, anesthetic category additions; counts/values not quantified

customer_additions: New European customers via CEP approvals (unnamed)
New customers in Asia on product launches
customers.customer_concentration: Not disclosed on this call (historically top-10 customers ~35-45% of revenue)
customers.net_assessment: Net positive — customer base broadening in regulated markets (Europe share to 14%), reducing historical LatAm/China-linked concentration

revenue_by_segment.Anesthetics: Largest therapeutic segment | strong FY26 growth (main driver)
revenue_by_segment.Antihistamines/vitamins/anti-hypertensiv: Steady traction | anti-allergics deliberately lower (regulated-market strategy)

segments.segment_outlook: Anesthetics + new cardio/ADHD launches accelerating; legacy antihistamine dependence continuing to dilute — a structural mix improvement story

revenue_mix.Exports: 82% (Europe 14% of total, LatAm/Asia the balance of exports)

geography.expansion_plans: Deeper Europe penetration via CEPs; US opportunity supported by clean USFDA status; no specific country timelines disclosed

headcount.utilization_target: Not disclosed; existing Lote Parshuram capacity ramping, Patalganga next

raw_materials.cost_outlook: Stable-to-favorable — backward integration insulates key starting materials; no adverse cost commentary in reviewed excerpts
raw_materials.expected_benefit: Not quantified; backward integration is the stated structural margin defense
raw_materials.key_materials: Key starting materials/intermediates (China-linked historically; integration reducing exposure)

working_capital.receivables_dso: Not split; total working capital days rose to 170 (from 158) in FY26
working_capital.cash_release_potential: Not disclosed; normalizing WC back to ~158 days would release roughly ₹25-30 Cr

capex_detailed.total: Not quantified in reviewed excerpts; Patalganga is the next major program (clearances secured)
capex_detailed.facility_expansion: Patalganga greenfield + Lote site module additions + finished-dosage capability
capex_detailed.funding_source: Internal accruals (company is effectively debt-free with strong cash generation)

debt_management.current_net_debt: Net cash position (historically); exact figure not disclosed in reviewed excerpts

market_share.by_segment: Global top-3 positions in several niche APIs (chlorpheniramine maleate, ketamine and select anesthetics/antihistamines historically #1-2 globally)
market_share.overall_trend: Strengthening — regulated-market share rising (Europe 14%), USFDA-clean status, and mix shifting to newer therapies

competitive_position.pricing_strategy: Niche-leadership pricing power in dominant molecules; regulated-market premiums; backward integration lets it remain cost-competitive vs Chinese API makers without sacrificing margin
competitive_advantages: Dominant global share in select niche APIs with decades of customer lock-in
Backward-integrated, USFDA/EU-GMP-cleared manufacturing (latest inspection: one minor observation)
Industry-leading profitability for an API company (35%+ EBITDA, ~25% PAT margins) with a debt-free balance sheet
competitive_threats: Chinese API competition in commodity-adjacent molecules (Medium)
Customer/molecule concentration in legacy antihistamines (Medium)
Regulatory action risk inherent to API plants — currently clean but always live (Low/Medium)

guidance.investor_verdict: A record Q4 (+50% revenue, +47% PAT) validated the new-product strategy and the ~20%/33-35% FY27 guide looks conservative against exit momentum — the watch items are merely working-capital creep (170 days) and whether Q4's surge borrowed from Q1.

analyst_dimensions.valuation_context: Stock jumped 20% to upper circuit on results from ₹580 (52-wk high ₹780, 1-yr return -20% pre-results); at ~₹700 post-rally on FY26 EPS ~₹26, trailing P/E ~27x and ~22x FY27E — reasonable for a 35%-margin, debt-free API franchise vs Divi's ~60x and mid-cap API peers 25-35x
analyst_dimensions.consensus_gap: Q4 decisively beat — the +20% circuit move is the market's verdict; the FY27 guide (~20%, 33-35%) is roughly in line with street hopes and arguably conservative vs the Q4 exit rate of +50%
analyst_dimensions.achievability: High — management has compounded revenue ~19% in FY26 against similar guidance and the FY27 drivers (launched products ramping, Europe CEPs, capacity available) are already visible rather than hoped-for; the margin band gives them room
analyst_dimensions.margin_quality: High quality: niche-molecule leadership + backward integration, not commodity-cycle luck; the slight Q4 margin dip (35.3% vs 36.7%) reflects mix/new-product ramp, and the 33-35% guide sensibly brackets absorption of new capacity
analyst_dimensions.capex_roi: Historical ROCE 20%+ on fully internal funding; Patalganga ROI depends on finished-dosage success — a margin-accretive but execution-heavier adjacency; the clean USFDA status materially de-risks regulated-market capex
analyst_dimensions.order_book_health: Not applicable; demand visibility rests on contracted regulated-market supply relationships and the disclosed strong traction in new launches
analyst_dimensions.risk_opportunity_scorecard: Opportunities: cardio/ADHD ramp (high prob/medium impact), Europe share gains via CEPs (high/medium), finished-dosage entry (medium/high), US expansion on clean FDA status (medium/medium). Risks: WC days drifting further (medium/low-medium), Q4 pull-forward normalizing Q1 (medium/low), China API price wars (medium/medium), molecule concentration (medium/medium)
analyst_dimensions.management_credibility: Strong — consistent ~20% guides delivered, transparent on the WC deterioration (self-flagged), clean audit/regulatory record, promoter-led (Wagh family) with focused strategy communication
analyst_dimensions.forward_estimate_impact: FY27E: revenue ~₹990-1,000 Cr (+20%), EBITDA ~₹330-350 Cr (34% mid), PAT ~₹235-255 Cr → EPS ~₹29-32 (+12-22%); street estimates likely revise up 5-8% post-beat, with upside if Q4's new-product momentum sustains rather than mean-reverts
analyst_dimensions.quarterly_trajectory: Q1 FY27 (Aug 2026) tests whether Q4 borrowed volume; H1: Europe CEP-driven customer ramps, Patalganga groundwork; H2: new-capacity contribution; catalysts — quarterly Europe/new-product disclosures, Patalganga capex announcement with numbers, any US customer wins, WC-days trajectory
